reason_for_classification: This video is a documentary or instructional piece from 'World Laparoscopy Hospital' showing surgical training and procedures in a teaching environment. The frames show multiple surgeons, students, and trainees in an operating room setting with decorative transitions and framing. No single trainee is performing a timed FLS task from the standardized FLS curriculum. The content is educational documentation, not a performance evaluation of a specific FLS module.
explanation: Per assessment instructions, instructional videos with educational/documentary context, multiple participants, and no isolated task performance are classified as 'instructional' and are not scored using the FLS rubric. This video does not show a single trainee performing one of the five standardized FLS tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture) under timed conditions with observable completion time and penalties.

demonstrated_techniques: Laparoscopic instrument handling in a teaching hospital environment
Surgical team coordination during laparoscopic procedures
Training environment setup for minimally invasive surgery
teaching_content.skill_level_demonstrated: mixed - shows teaching context rather than single-performance demonstration
teaching_content.context: Documentary of World Laparoscopy Hospital training program showing multiple participants and general laparoscopic surgical environment

cannot_score_reasons: Multiple participants - no single trainee isolated for performance scoring
No clear task identification from standardized FLS curriculum
No visible timing or task-specific setup (Peg Transfer board, Pattern Cut gauze, Endoloop foam, Penrose drain, etc.)
Decorative framing and transitions indicate documentary/instructional intent rather than task performance
Multiple scene transitions suggest multiple different procedures/teaching moments, not one task
